summary: Refactored Kirby's motion-state table and related fighter motion flag definitions away from raw numeric constants and enum-typed flag groups toward named `static u32 const ... ATTRIBUTE_USED` bitmask constants. The main payoff was making `ftKb_Init_MotionStateTable` searchable and maintainable by replacing hex flag fields and move-id values with symbolic `ftKb_MF_*` and `FtMoveId_*` expressions, while standardizing similar motion-flag constants across many fighter `forward.h` headers.
what_changed: Added a large set of Kirby-specific motion flag constants to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/forward.h`, including `ftKb_MF_MultiJump`, `ftKb_MF_AttackDash`, many copied neutral-B variants such as `ftKb_MF_SpecialNMr`, `ftKb_MF_SpecialNFx`, `ftKb_MF_SpecialNGw`, and their air/loop variants.

Updated `ftKb_Init.c` to include `forward.h` and replace raw motion-state table bitmasks like `0x0000006A` and packed move-id values like `0x01000000` with named constants such as `ftKb_MF_MultiJump` and `FtMoveId_Unk01 << 24`.

Converted many fighter `*_MotionFlags` enum definitions into `static u32 const ... ATTRIBUTE_USED` constants in fighter `forward.h` files, including Captain, CLink, DrMario, Fox, GameWatch, Link, Peach, Donkey, and others listed in the PR file set.
Changed several local transition flag declarations from `Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags const` to `u32 const`, for example in Captain special move files and other fighter special files.
Preserved existing bitwise compositions of `FtStateChange_*` flags while making the intermediate combinations reusable and directly named.
smart_moves: Used symbolic bitmask composition instead of leaving large opaque hex values in Kirby's motion table; this makes future motion-state auditing much easier.
Introduced factorized base constants such as `ftKb_MF_18_20_21`, `ftKb_MF_4_18_20_21`, and `ftKb_MF_5_18_20_21` so repeated Kirby flag combinations only need to be reasoned about once.
Applied `ATTRIBUTE_USED` to header-level `static u32 const` flag constants so constants can live in headers without being dropped or triggering unused issues in build configurations.
Standardized flag constants as `u32` rather than enum types or `Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags`, matching the underlying use as bitmask values in motion-state data.
Kept air-state constants mechanically related to ground-state constants by adding `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`, e.g. `ftKb_MF_SpecialAirN = ftKb_MF_SpecialN | FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`.
decomp_lessons: When cleaning motion-state tables, first name the flag masks and move-id fields, then replace raw table literals; this improves readability without changing behavior.
For bitmask constants that combine other bitmask constants, `static u32 const` is safer and clearer than enum members when the values are not conceptually a closed enumeration.
Header-level motion flag definitions need careful linkage/usage treatment; this PR's pattern is `static u32 const <fighter>_MF_<Name> ATTRIBUTE_USED = ...;`.
Copied-character systems such as Kirby benefit from explicit per-source-fighter suffixes in constants because many similar neutral-B states differ only by a few `FtStateChange_*` bits.
Mechanical refactors across all fighter headers help keep per-character cleanup consistent, but the evidence shows no human review discussion explaining hidden matching constraints.
naming_conventions: `ftKb_MF_*` names are used for Kirby motion flag constants.
`MF` abbreviates motion flags in fighter headers, e.g. `ftCa_MF_SpecialN`, `ftFx_MF_SpecialAirLwLoop`, `ftDk_MF_CargoThrowF`.

Kirby copied neutral-B flags use source-fighter suffixes such as `Mr`, `Kp`, `Pe`, `Ys`, `Lg`, `Zd`, `Dr`, `Gk`, `Fx`, `Lk`, `Sk`, `Ns`, `Pp`, `Pk`, `Ss`, `Mt`, `Cl`, `Fc`, `Pc`, `Gw`, `Ca`, `Dk`, `Pr`, `Ms`, `Gn`, and `Fe`.
Air variants are named by inserting `Air`, e.g. `ftKb_MF_SpecialN` to `ftKb_MF_SpecialAirN`.
Loop and charged variants keep semantic suffixes such as `Loop`, `Charged`, `Start`, and `Rebound`.

Some factorized constants use bit-position-style names such as `ftKb_MF_18_20_21`; these are explicitly marked by a nearby `@todo Clean up factorization`, so they are a transitional convention rather than a final semantic naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced literal bitmasks with named constants while preserving the exact bitwise `FtStateChange_*` composition, a low-risk cleanup tactic for matching-sensitive motion tables.
Used `FtMoveId_Unk01 << 24` style expressions to express packed move-id fields instead of raw shifted hex constants.

Changed flag constant types to `u32 const`, likely to reflect the actual table field width and avoid enum/type artifacts; the diff evidence supports the type change but does not include reviewer rationale.
Kept constants `static` in headers to avoid external symbol emission while using `ATTRIBUTE_USED` to keep compiler behavior stable.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title, file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the `@todo Clean up factorization` in `ftKirby/forward.h`.
Search for additional replacements of raw motion-state table hex constants with `FtStateChange_*` and `FtMoveId_*` names.
Compare later conventions for `Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags` versus `u32` in motion flag declarations.
Look for PRs that rename numeric-style constants such as `ftKb_MF_18_20_21` to semantic names.
